params.name: patient
params.description: FHIR logical id of the beneficiary Patient resource.

params.name: type
params.description: Claim type filter: carrier, pde, dme, hha, hospice, inpatient, outpatient, snf.

params.name: service-date
params.description: Filter by service date with gt/lt/ge/le prefixes.

params.name: _lastUpdated
params.description: Filter by last_updated date with gt/lt prefixes.

params.name: excludeSAMHSA
params.description: Set true to exclude SAMHSA-related claims.

items.docs: Returns Medicare Parts A, B, and D claims as CARIN Blue Button ExplanationOfBenefit resources in a FHIR Bundle.

info.name: Read a single claim by id

params.name: id
params.description: FHIR logical id of the ExplanationOfBenefit.

info.name: Search the authorized beneficiary's Patient resource

params.name: _id
params.description: FHIR logical id of the Patient resource.

params.name: _lastUpdated
params.description: Filter by last_updated date with gt/lt prefixes.

items.docs: Returns the beneficiary's demographic record as a Patient resource in a FHIR Bundle. Available only if the beneficiary shared demographic data during authorization.

info.name: Read the beneficiary's Patient resource by id

params.name: id
params.description: FHIR logical id of the Patient resource.

items.docs: Returns the Patient resource for the authorizing beneficiary.

params.name: beneficiary
params.description: FHIR logical id of the beneficiary Patient resource.

params.name: _lastUpdated
params.description: Filter by last_updated date with gt/lt prefixes.

items.docs: Returns Coverage resources in a FHIR Bundle - one per Medicare coverage type (Part A, Part B, Part D).

info.name: Read a single Coverage resource by id

params.name: id
params.description: FHIR logical id of the Coverage resource.

items.docs: Returns the server CapabilityStatement (FHIR 4.0.1). No authorization required.
Returns the authorizing beneficiary's OpenID Connect profile (name, family name, email) when openid and profile scopes were granted.
Beneficiary-facing authorization endpoint. The user logs in with Medicare.gov credentials and chooses whether to share demographic data. PKCE S256 is mandatory.
Exchanges the authorization code (plus PKCE code_verifier) for a 1-hour access token. Refresh tokens are one-time-use and issued only to approved 13-month and research application types.
